behaviors.description: Products marked as out-of-stock are excluded from recommendation results regardless of relevance score
behaviors.justification: Unit test with mocked products would verify filtering logic, but real failures live in data loading: Does the query actually fetch the availability flag? Is it named correctly? A mocked product list wouldn't catch schema mismatches or database query bugs. Integration testing with real product data and availability checks catches these.
behaviors.test_description: Load a test database with products marked available and out-of-stock. Generate recommendations for a user with high affinities to out-of-stock items. Verify out-of-stock products are absent from results even if their relevance scores are high.

behaviors.description: User preference filter correctly excludes categories blocked in user settings
behaviors.justification: Filtering logic could work in unit tests, but real failures occur in user settings loading: Does the query fetch blocked categories? Are they stored in the right table? Is the join correct? Mocks hide these bugs. Integration testing verifies user preferences are loaded and applied correctly.
behaviors.test_description: Create a user with explicit category blocks (e.g., 'Electronics' blocked). Load a test database with recommendations spanning blocked and allowed categories. Verify no products from blocked categories appear in results.

behaviors.description: Recommendation algorithm ranks products matching recent browsing patterns higher than products with no browsing signal
behaviors.justification: Ranking logic could be unit-tested with mock browsing histories, but real failures occur in data loading and weighting: Is browsing history fetched correctly? Are recency weights applied correctly? Do database queries return the right signals? Integration testing with real user browsing data and loaded weights catches these.
behaviors.test_description: Create a user with detailed browsing history. Generate recommendations. Verify that products from recently browsed categories appear in top positions, and products with zero browsing signal are ranked lower (or missing entirely if other filters apply).

behaviors.description: Pagination returns correct page with stable ordering—requesting page 2 twice returns identical results if underlying data hasn't changed
behaviors.justification: Pagination offset logic could be unit-tested, but real failures occur in database ordering: Without explicit ORDER BY, database queries can return different orderings on successive calls. Mocks with in-memory lists would hide this. Integration testing with a real or representative database catches ordering instability.
behaviors.test_description: Generate a full recommendation list, then request page 2 twice with identical parameters and no intervening data changes. Verify the product IDs and positions are identical across both page 2 requests.

behaviors.description: Algorithm surfaces at least one product from a category user hasn't browsed before (serendipity), avoiding filter bubble
behaviors.justification: Unit test could verify the serendipity logic exists, but real failure is the guarantee not holding at scale: Does the algorithm actually find products in unbrowsed categories? Does it fail when all top products happen to be from visited categories? Integration testing with realistic product data and user histories catches algorithmic gaps.
behaviors.test_description: Create a user who has browsed 9 of 10 product categories intensively. Generate recommendations. Verify at least one product from the unbrowsed category appears in results. Test edge case: user with very high affinity scores across visited categories—serendipity should still appear.

behaviors.description: New user with no purchase or browsing history receives recommendations based on overall popularity, not empty results
behaviors.justification: Fallback logic could be unit-tested, but real failures occur in popularity data loading: Is popularity calculated correctly? Is it loaded for new users? Are popularity scores reasonable? Mocks could return fake popularity data. Integration testing with real product popularity data catches these.
behaviors.test_description: Create a new user with zero purchase and browsing history. Generate recommendations. Verify results contain popular products (not empty), with top results matching known high-popularity products from the catalog.

behaviors.description: Click tracking records correct product ID, position in list, and timestamp for each recommendation
behaviors.justification: Recording a click could work in unit tests, but real failures occur in position calculation context: Is position calculated as list_index correctly? If pagination changed between recommendation generation and click, is the position still valid? Timestamp accuracy depends on system time. Integration testing with actual pagination state and timing catches these.
behaviors.test_description: Generate recommendations (page 1, 10 items). Click product at position 3. Record the click. Verify the stored record contains: correct product_id, position=3, and a reasonable timestamp. Repeat for page 2 clicks to verify position is relative to page, not absolute.

behaviors.description: Response time for generating recommendations stays under 200ms for a catalog of 100,000 products
behaviors.justification: Performance requirements must be tested with realistic data at scale. Unit tests with small mock datasets would pass but miss real bottlenecks. Integration testing with 100k products in a test database (or realistic snapshot) measures the actual algorithmic and database query performance.
behaviors.test_description: Load a test database with 100,000 products, realistic user browsing/purchase history, and category distributions. Generate 50 independent recommendation requests for various user profiles. Measure end-to-end latency from request to full results. Verify 95th percentile ≤ 200ms.

behaviors.description: When recommended product is purchased, algorithm deprioritizes it in subsequent requests
behaviors.justification: Deprioritization logic could be unit-tested, but real failures occur in purchase history updates: Is the purchase recorded in the right table? Is it fetched on next recommendation query? Is deprioritization weighting applied? Mocks with fake purchase records would hide query and update bugs. Integration testing with real purchase history and ranking interaction catches these.
behaviors.test_description: Generate recommendations for a user. Identify a recommended product and record a purchase event. Generate new recommendations immediately and after a small delay. Verify the purchased product is absent or significantly ranked lower than before.

behaviors.description: Recommendation results are deterministic—requesting twice with same user state returns same ranked list
behaviors.justification: Determinism could be unit-tested if random seeds are controlled, but real failures are database/float non-determinism: Does the database return rows in the same order every time? Are floating-point comparisons in ranking deterministic? Integration testing with real database queries and actual ranking logic (not mocked) catches these.
behaviors.test_description: Generate recommendations for a user. Record the full ranked list (product IDs in order). Wait a brief moment, then generate recommendations again with identical user state. Verify the new list is identical—same products in same order. Repeat 5 times to catch rare non-determinism.
